title: Getting started with quokkajs
body: quokkajs provides an RTK Query-style API for React without requiring Redux. Define your endpoints once and get fully typed hooks back automatically.

title: How tag-based cache invalidation works
body: When a mutation succeeds, quokkajs finds all cached query entries whose providesTags overlap with the mutation's invalidatesTags and re-triggers them automatically.

title: SHA-256 request deduplication in the cache
body: Every cached response is keyed by a SHA-256 hash of the fully-resolved request parameters with keys recursively sorted, ensuring parameter-order independence.

title: Using prepareHeaders with Zustand
body: Pass your store's getState to QuokkaProvider and read the auth token inside prepareHeaders. The token is injected into every request made by that API.

title: Debounced search with fetchOnArgsChange
body: Combine fetchOnArgsChange with debouncedDuration to build search-as-you-type UIs that only fire a network request after the user stops typing.

title: Polling and focus refetch patterns
body: Set pollingInterval to keep data fresh on a timer, and refetchOnFocus to immediately re-fetch when the user returns to the browser tab.

title: The useFetch hook for one-off requests
body: useFetch is a lower-level hook that works without createApi and QuokkaProvider. Use it for standalone requests that don't belong to a named API slice.

title: TypeScript inference with MakeHook
body: The MakeHook mapped type derives use${Capitalize<K>}${Query|Mutation} signatures at compile time, giving full type inference with zero manual annotation.
